Examination: Tooth 27 is temporarily sealed.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Pulpitis in tooth 27.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Isolation was applied to tooth 27, temporary seal removed, canal irrigated, dried, and master cone trialed to working length. The canal was cleaned and dried, then filled with SP and gutta-percha points. The base was lined and temporarily sealed with GIC.
Medical Instructions: Return if any discomfort occurs.

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Deep caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x008).
Examination: Tooth 27 has a temporary crown in place with no soft tissue abnormalities. Teeth 11, 12, 21, and 22 show brown cavitated lesions with soft texture and dark color, with some existing fillings. Teeth 13 and 23 have brown discoloration on the mesial surfaces with soft probing.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: * Tooth 27: Deep caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x008).
* Teeth 11, 12, 21, 22: Secondary caries (K02.800x001).
* Teeth 13, 23: Moderate caries in permanent teeth (K02.800x007).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: For tooth 27, the patient was informed of the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis and consented to treatment. The temporary crown and sealing materials were removed, the abutment and restoration were cleaned, and the restoration was tried in for fit and proximal contact. It was cemented with dual-cure resin cement, occlusion was adjusted, and polished. For teeth 11, 12, 21, and 22, local infiltration anesthesia with compound articaine was administered, caries were removed, and composite resin fillings were placed. Veneer preparation and polishing were performed, and an upper jaw model was taken for restoration fabrication. For teeth 13 and 23, caries removal, cavity preparation, etching, drying, bonding agent application, and composite filling were performed, with occlusion adjustment and polishing. The patient was advised on care instructions.
Medical Instructions: Avoid biting hard foods, maintain oral hygiene, and return for follow-up. If pulpal symptoms occur, root canal therapy may be needed.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Post-root canal therapy; dental arch defect.
Examination: Tooth 35 is temporarily sealed, with normal mucosa in the periapical area and no percussion pain. Teeth 44 and 45 have occlusal restorations, normal periapical mucosa, and no percussion pain. Gingival healing is good in the implant areas of teeth 24, 25, 26, and 36, with no significant redness or swelling. Oral hygiene is fair.
Radiographic Examination: Teeth 44 and 45 show root canal fillings with no obvious periapical abnormalities.
Diagnosis: Post-root canal therapy for teeth 35, 44, and 45; dental arch defect.
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed of the pros and cons of various restorative materials, costs, timeframes, potential follow-up visits, and complications. The patient chose full ceramic crowns. For tooth 35, a post space was prepared, a fiber post was placed, resin was used for core build-up, the abutment was prepared, and a silicone impression and bite registration were taken. A temporary crown was fabricated. Shade matching was performed. For teeth 24, 25, 26, and 36, impressions were taken for transitional dentures.
Medical Instructions: Advise the patient to rest and avoid consuming excessively cold, hot, hard, or sticky foods. If any loosening or discomfort occurs, they should return for a follow-up appointment, scheduling by phone in advance.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling.
Examination: Generalized gingival redness and swelling with easy bleeding on probing, no periodontal pockets detected. Presence of calculus (++), pigmentation (+).
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Chronic gingivitis (K05.151).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed of the diagnosis, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to treatment. Full-mouth supragingival ultrasonic scaling was performed, followed by fluoride varnish application and iodine glycerin. Oral hygiene instructions (OHI) were provided, including brushing, flossing, and interdental brush use.
Medical Instructions: Avoid acidic foods and extreme temperatures for one week. Maintain oral hygiene with mouthwash rinses. Regular check-ups and follow-up if discomfort occurs.

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Initial consultation. Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105). Treatment: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Examination: Generalized gingival redness and swelling with easy bleeding on probing. No periodontal pockets detected. Food impaction (++), calculus (+), and pigmentation (+) present.
Radiographic Examination: No radiographs taken.
Diagnosis: Gingivitis (K05.105).
Treatment Plan: Full-mouth supragingival scaling.
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: The patient was informed about the condition, treatment plan, costs, and prognosis, and consented to the treatment. Air polishing, full-mouth supragingival scaling, and polishing were performed. Enamel sealing, steam eye mask application, and iodine glycerin application were completed. Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Partial edentulism (K08.100x002).
Examination: Teeth 11, 21, and 31 are missing with no gingival abnormalities.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Partial edentulism of teeth 11, 21, and 31 (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Removable partial dentures were adjusted and fitted for teeth 11, 21, and 31. Retention was good with no rocking. Occlusion was adjusted and polished.
Medical Instructions: 1. Initial denture wear may cause foreign body sensation, including nausea, increased salivation, and speech difficulties, which will improve with practice. 2. Avoid biting hard foods in the first 1-2 weeks; start with small, soft foods and gradually increase hardness and size. 3. Learn the correct method for inserting and removing dentures, aligning them properly and pushing gently into place. Avoid excessive force or biting to prevent damage. 4. Clean dentures after meals and soak them in water overnight. Use denture cleaning tablets regularly. 5. Maintain oral hygiene of remaining teeth with proper brushing and regular cleanings.
Remarks:

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013). Treatment: Align and level the dental arches, flatten the Spee curve, correct crossbite, expand the dental arch, advance the maxillary arch, inhibit mandibular growth, and increase posterior teeth height using invisible aligners (Angel Aligner Kid).
Examination: Occlusion: Normal; Overjet: Anterior crossbite. Skeletal Class III with low mandibular plane angle. Anterior open bite: Normal. Dental crowding: Maxillary arch 2 degrees, mandibular arch I degree. Occlusal plane: Normal. Arch form: Maxillary oval, mandibular oval. Palate: High arch. Lingual frenulum: Acceptable. Mandibular retrusion: Present. Oral hygiene: Fair. Oral mucosa: Normal. Tongue: Normal. Speech: Normal. Midlines: Upper and lower normal. Jaw structure: Maxilla and mandible normal. Alveolar bone: Maxilla and mandible underdeveloped. Facial symmetry: Present. Profile: Concave. Facial height: Short. Lip-tooth relationship: Normal. Upper lip: Full. Lower lip: Full.
Radiographic Examination: 
Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013).

Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Partial edentulism (K08.100x002).
Examination: Teeth 34, 35, and 36 are missing with a mesiodistal gap of 25.5 mm and an occlusogingival distance of 5 mm. The alveolar ridge is resorbed with no supraeruption of opposing teeth. Overall oral hygiene is fair with presence of calculus and staining. No significant occlusal or joint abnormalities noted.
Radiographic Examination: Radiographs show alveolar bone width of 5.1 mm and height of 11 mm in the area of missing teeth 34, 35, and 36.
Diagnosis: Partial edentulism of teeth 34, 35, and 36 (K08.100x002).
Treatment Plan: 
Treatment Recommendations: 
Management: Informed consent for implant restoration was obtained. Preoperative antibiotics were administered. Local anesthesia with articaine was used. A horizontal incision was made at the crest of the ridge, and a full-thickness flap was reflected. Osteotomy sites were prepared, and Dentium implants were placed: 3.4x10 mm for site 34 and 4.3x8 mm for site 36. Cover screws were placed, bone graft material was applied, and the flap was sutured with tension-free closure. Postoperative radiographs confirmed implant positioning.
Medical Instructions: Strict adherence to post-implant care instructions is required. Oral antibiotics should be taken as prescribed. Follow-up for suture removal in two weeks.
Remarks:

ground_truth:
Patient Record: Follow-up consultation. Diagnosis: Angle’s Class III malocclusion (K07.200x013). Treatment: Align and level the dental arches, flatten the Spee curve, correct intermaxillary relations, expand the dental arch, distalize molars, advance the maxillary arch, inhibit mandibular growth, increase anterior and posterior tooth height, perform interproximal enamel reduction. Treatment method: Invisible aligners.
Examination: Normal overbite and overjet. Skeletal Class III with a normal mandibular plane angle. Normal anterior open bite. Normal dental arch crowding. Normal occlusal plane. Maxillary arch is ovoid, mandibular arch is square. High palatal vault. Acceptable lingual frenulum attachment. Retrusive mandible. General oral hygiene is average, with normal oral mucosa, tongue, and speech. Normal upper and lower midlines. Normal maxilla and mandible. Alveolar bone is underdeveloped in both arches.
